about.description:
The information presented in this app about Arkham Horror: The Card Game™, both textual and graphical, is © Fantasy Flight Games {{date}}. This app is a fan project and is not produced, endorsed, or supported by, or affiliated with Fantasy Flight Games.

This application was created by Felix and contributors to support the Arkham Horror: The Card Game community. The source code of this project is available at Github. Feedback and bug reports are welcome via Github issues or the dedicated channel on the Mythos Busters discord server.

All artwork and illustrations are the intellectual property of their respective creators. All Arkham Horror: The Card Game™ images and graphics are copyrighted by Fantasy Flight Games.

translation.title: This app is available in multiple languages thanks to the following contributors

hall_of_fame.zzorba: Access to the ArkhamCards API and icons, assistance with questions and inspiration for the deckbuilder. Without you this project would not have been possible.

deck_collection.import_arkhamdb_help: Enter a public ArkhamDB deck id, deck url or deck guide url. Decks will be copied over and deck history and upgrade state will not be imported. If you are meaning to sync your ArkhamDB decks with this app, connect your arkhamdb account.
deck_collection.title: Decks

deck_create.help_advanced:
Signature cards with "Advanced" are stronger versions of an investigator's signature cards that are listed under "Deck Requirements".

At any point during a campaign (including at deck creation) you may optionally choose to include these advanced signature cards instead of the original signature cards.
deck_create.help_replacements:
Signature cards with "Replacement" can replace the signature cards that are listed under "Deckbuilding Requirements". Doing so still satisfies the requirement and the deck is valid to play.

If doing so, both signature cards must be replaced and if doing so for a campaign, you cannot later change to using the original signature cards.

Alternatively, the "Replacement" cards can be included in addition to the signature cards.
deck_create.title: Create {{name}} deck

actions.draft_random_basic_weakness: Draft random basic weakness (Boon of the Morrígan)

card_pool.campaign_playalong_help: The Campaign Play-Along is an ongoing event hosted on the Mythos Busters discord server. It is designed to play Arkham campaigns together, share stories and generally create a sense of community.
card_pool.collection_help: Set your current <settings_link>collection</settings_link> as this deck's card pool. This can be useful to freeze the deck's card pool, or to share a "build preset" with people sharing a physical collection.
card_pool.current_faq25_help: When deckbuilding in the Current Environment, players may only deckbuild with core cards, the five starter decks and the three most recent investigator expansions.
card_pool.current_help: When deckbuilding in the the Current environment, players may use the following products: The 2026 core set, the five investigator decks, the two most recently released deckbuilding expansions and the two most recently released investigator expansions after the 2026 core set.
card_pool.help: Investigators, signature cards and story assets are not affected by this selection.
card_pool.legacy_help: When deckbuilding in the Legacy Environment, players may build their decks and use investigators, player cards, and weaknesses from any Arkham Horror: The Card Game™ product. There are no restrictions whatsoever.
card_pool.limited_faq25_help: When deckbuilding in the Limited Environment, players may only build their decks with cards from the core set, the five starter decks and three different investigator expansions of their choice.
card_pool.limited_help: When deckbuilding in the the Limited environment, players may use the following products: The 2026 core set, the five investigator decks, three different player card expansions of their choice.
card_pool.substitute_chapter_2_with_chapter_1: Substitute chapter 2 core and investigator decks with chapter 1 equivalents
card_pool.title: Limited card pool

sealed.help: Upload a sealed deck definition (.csv) to use it. Use ArkhamSealed to generate a sealed deck.
sealed.title: Sealed

deck_edit.create_local_copy_help: Create a local copy of this deck. This will apply all outstanding edits and allow you to save them. Upgrades and deck history will not be carried over.
deck_edit.discard_confirm: Are you sure you want to discard your changes?

draft_weakness_modal.explanation_body: The Boon of the Morrígan gives players more control over the basic weakness in comparison to fully randomizing it. Three basic weaknesses are drawn at random, the player gets to cancel one of them and then one of the remaining two weaknesses is added to the deck at random.
draft_weakness_modal.title: Drafting a basic weakness
draft_weakness_modal.too_few_rbw: Too few random basic weaknesses available to perform draft.

notes.banner_url_help: Banner image for the deck. Will be displayed on the deck view and in social media previews. Aspect ratio is 4:1, optimal width is 1440px.
notes.intro_help: A short deck introduction. Will be displayed on the deck view and in social media previews. Supports ArkhamDB-flavoured markdown.

quick_upgrade.shrewd_analysis_help: The app will add an XP adjustment matching the received discount.

recommendations.core_help: Marking a card as "Core" tells the recommendation engine to only consider decks that contain it.
recommendations.relative_help: Shows which cards {{name}} is more likely to use than other investigators, ranked by percentile.
recommendations.tooltip_relative: {{investigator}} uses {{name}} more often than {{percentile}}% of all investigators.

draw_simulator.mulligan_mode_help: When enabled, non-Tarot weaknesses and cards that start in play are redrawn automatically. After mulligan mode is disabled, the deck is shuffled once.
draw_simulator.redraw_tooltip: Selected cards will be appended to the end of the deck and then replaced with new cards.

draw_simulator.reshuffle_tooltip: Selected cards will be shuffled back into the deck.
draw_simulator.title: Draw simulator

actions.show_limited_access_help: Investigators may have limited, optional access to cards based on level, class, trait, type, and/or other criteria. When this is enabled, cards from that limited access will be shown.

popular_decks.error: Popular deck guides could not be loaded: {{error}}

preview_banner.description: Enabling previews will show cards from upcoming sets in the card browser. You can change this setting at any time in the collection settings.
preview_banner.title: Preview season is here!

grimoire.intro_description: The Arkham Grimoire is a comprehensive collection of rulings covering all known situations in the game. As a living document, it is maintained online and updated as new cards, rules, and interactions are introduced or discovered. This document contains information pertaining to more advanced topics such as the interpretation of card text, the resolution of timing conflicts, frequently asked questions, and a list of all keywords and other rules text.

delete.help: Permanently delete your arkham.build account and associated data. This deletes server-side credentials, sessions, account-synced decks, settings, and OAuth connections. Account-linked decks are removed from this device. Device-only decks, settings, and other browser data is not deleted. Content synced to ArkhamDB is not deleted from ArkhamDB.
delete.title: Delete account

display.font_size_preview: Armitage had an idea that the alphabet might be something esoterically used by certain forbidden cults which have come down from old times, and which have inherited many forms and traditions from the wizards of the Saracenic world.

display.show_move_to_side_deck_help: This shows a button that allows you to move cards from the deck to the side deck when editing decks. This is helpful if you primarily deck build by overfilling the deck with options and then cutting down (known as the dayorbyte method).
display.sort_ignore_punctuation_help: When this is checked, the card list will ignore quotes and other punctuation when sorting.

suzi_standalone_setup.description: FAQ 2.4 defines how standalone decks for Suzi may be built. Random cards between level 1-5 from your collection are drawn until your desired amount of XP is reached. Afterwards, an equivalent number of cards have to be removed from the deck.
